name: enum34
documentation:
enum --- support for enumerations
========================================

An enumeration is a set of symbolic names (members) bound to unique, constant
values.  Within an enumeration, the members can be compared by identity, and
the enumeration itself can be iterated over.

    from enum import Enum

    class Fruit(Enum):
        apple = 1
        banana = 2
        orange = 3

    list(Fruit)
    # [<Fruit.apple: 1>, <Fruit.banana: 2>, <Fruit.orange: 3>]

    len(Fruit)
    # 3

    Fruit.banana
    # <Fruit.banana: 2>

    Fruit['banana']
    # <Fruit.banana: 2>

    Fruit(2)
    # <Fruit.banana: 2>

    Fruit.banana is Fruit['banana'] is Fruit(2)
    # True

    Fruit.banana.name
    # 'banana'

    Fruit.banana.value
    # 2

Repository and Issue Tracker at https://bitbucket.org/stoneleaf/enum34.

modules.name: enum
documentation: Python Enumerations

functions.name: enum._is_descriptor
documentation: Returns True if obj is a descriptor, False otherwise.

functions.name: enum._is_dunder
documentation: Returns True if a __dunder__ name, False otherwise.

functions.name: enum._is_sunder
documentation: Returns True if a _sunder_ name, False otherwise.

functions.name: enum._make_class_unpicklable
documentation: Make the given class un-picklable.

functions.name: enum.name
documentation: _RouteClassAttributeToGetattr is used to provide access to the `name`
and `value` properties of enum members while keeping some measure of
protection from modification, while still allowing for an enumeration
to have members named `name` and `value`.  This works because enumeration
members are not set directly on the enum class -- __getattr__ is
used to look them up.

functions.name: enum._convert
documentation: Create a new Enum subclass that replaces a collection of global constants

functions.name: enum.unique
documentation: Class decorator that ensures only unique members exist in an enumeration.

classes.name: enum._RouteClassAttributeToGetattr
documentation:
Route attribute access on a class to __getattr__.

This is a descriptor, used to define attributes that act differently when
accessed through an instance and through a class.  Instance access remains
normal, but access to an attribute through a class will be routed to the
class's __getattr__ method; this is done by raising AttributeError.

classes.name: enum._EnumDict

methods.name: enum._EnumDict.__setitem__
documentation:
Changes anything not dundered or not a descriptor.

If a descriptor is added with the same name as an enum member, the name
is removed from _member_names (this may leave a hole in the numerical
sequence of values).

If an enum member name is used twice, an error is raised; duplicate
values are not checked for.

Single underscore (sunder) names are reserved.

Note:   in 3.x __order__ is simply discarded as a not necessary piece
        leftover from 2.x

Track enum member order and ensure member names are not reused.

EnumMeta will use the names found in self._member_names as the
enumeration member names.

classes.name: enum.EnumMeta

methods.name: enum.EnumMeta.__bool__
documentation: classes/types should always be True.

methods.name: enum.EnumMeta.__call__
documentation:
Either returns an existing member, or creates a new enum class.

This method is used both when an enum class is given a value to match
to an enumeration member (i.e. Color(3)) and for the functional API
(i.e. Color = Enum('Color', names='red green blue')).

When used for the functional API: `module`, if set, will be stored in
the new class' __module__ attribute; `type`, if set, will be mixed in
as the first base class.

Note: if `module` is not set this routine will attempt to discover the
calling module by walking the frame stack; if this is unsuccessful
the resulting class will not be pickleable.

methods.name: enum.EnumMeta.__members__
documentation:
Returns a mapping of member name->value.

This mapping lists all enum members, including aliases. Note that this
is a copy of the internal mapping.

methods.name: enum.EnumMeta.__getattr__
documentation:
Return the enum member matching `name`

We use __getattr__ instead of descriptors or inserting into the enum
class' __dict__ in order to support `name` and `value` being both
properties for enum members (which live in the class' __dict__) and
enum members themselves.

methods.name: enum.EnumMeta.__setattr__
documentation:
Block attempts to reassign Enum members.

A simple assignment to the class namespace only changes one of the
several possible ways to get an Enum member from the Enum class,
resulting in an inconsistent Enumeration.

methods.name: enum.EnumMeta._create_
documentation:
Convenience method to create a new Enum class.

`names` can be:

* A string containing member names, separated either with spaces or
  commas.  Values are auto-numbered from 1.
* An iterable of member names.  Values are auto-numbered from 1.
* An iterable of (member name, value) pairs.
* A mapping of member name -> value.

methods.name: enum.EnumMeta._get_mixins_
documentation:
Returns the type for creating enum members, and the first inherited
enum class.

bases: the tuple of bases that was given to __new__

methods.name: enum.EnumMeta._find_new_
documentation:
Returns the __new__ to be used for creating the enum members.

classdict: the class dictionary given to __new__
member_type: the data type whose __new__ will be used by default
first_enum: enumeration to check for an overriding __new__

methods.name: enum.EnumMeta._find_new_
documentation:
Returns the __new__ to be used for creating the enum members.

classdict: the class dictionary given to __new__
member_type: the data type whose __new__ will be used by default
first_enum: enumeration to check for an overriding __new__
Metaclass for Enum

documentation: Enum where members are also (and must be) ints
